key_points: Nusbaum apartments are sought-after; students share photos publicly
Campus lighting failures reported near Webb creating safety concerns
Limited student discussion of dorm quality beyond specific buildings
key_quotes: I lived there for two years and am happy to answer any questions
They turned the lights off at the back of Webb... I almost ran someone over on a lime because of the blindness

key_points: Active student community with clubs, ROTC, and campus events
Fraternities and sports culture present but not dominant in discussion
Campus engagement drops sharply for online-only students
key_quotes: Would there be any interest in a DND club?
I never made any networking connections while at ODU because of social anxiety

key_points: Meal plan costs $7,000 — students feel ripped off for quality received
Webb Chick-fil-A had repeated reports of smoke-smelling chicken
Food quality reportedly declines mid-semester, improves near renewal time
key_quotes: They charge so much just for the quality to sharply decline mid-semester and conveniently improve towards the end when it's time to buy meal plans
I spent money on it and I'm seriously regretting it because I think I have food poisoning now

key_points: Campus shooting left students, faculty, and remote students deeply traumatized
SPCA therapy animals and local businesses offered free counseling after shooting
Administration's cold, lawyer-drafted responses worsened student distress
key_quotes: I kinda wanted to just go to class and process shit that way
There's no genuine compassion or care or concern in any of the messages. They all read like ChatGPT
I need a friend to come... I don't want to be alone right now

key_points: Scholarships available; some students report ODU paying them to attend
Graduation regalia costs $140 for one-time use — students frustrated
8-week course switch raises cost concerns for part-time working students
key_quotes: They also are paying me to attend after all of my scholarships
Crazy to me that I paid $140 for the cap and gown that I will wear once
So those of us who work full time just don't matter?

key_points: Forced switch to 8-week online courses widely condemned by students and faculty
Faculty senate voted 41-7 no confidence in president over course compression
Half of professors on RateMyProfessor rated under 2 stars per student posts
key_quotes: 8 week classes is not a good idea, too pushy and rushy especially if STEM majors
Absolutely not like half the professors I've seen at this school have a rate my professor rating under 2
Asynchronous online courses are an absolute joke. You just get thrown the textbook with little to no guidance

key_points: President Hemphill received 5-year extension despite 41-7 faculty no-confidence vote
Board of Visitors accused of gaslighting students, faculty, and ignoring shared governance
Weeks to get advisor responses; account holds used to force criminal history survey
key_quotes: Been here for 3.5 years after 20 years in actual universities and this is the most incompetent and pathetic excuse for a school I've seen
There is no leadership, just cookie cutter responses to deny liability for anything and constantly gaslight students and faculty
If you are a freshman or a sophomore, get out of this while you can

key_points: Norfolk campus has city feel; proximity to Hampton Roads military community
Campus security is severely inadequate — unlocked arena, poor lighting, open access
Parking garage confusion and poor signage are ongoing daily complaints
key_quotes: Being able to just walk into Chartway and access some rooms is insane. ODU needs to step shit up badly
Diversity and city feel! Also loved that their cybersecurity program was solid
I used to walk in the VIP way to get coffee — basically nothing was locked

key_points: ODU alumni network actively helps graduates get hired, especially in Hampton Roads
Hiring managers who are ODU alums give ODU grads automatic interview consideration
LinkedIn networking from ODU can open doors even without on-campus connections
key_quotes: As a hiring manager and ODU alum, an ODU grad almost always gets an interview from me
I got rejected from 50+ jobs since I graduated. This job was the first interview I got and I was hired a few days later
Networking can be as simple as running your LinkedIn account

key_points: 8-week course compression seen as money grab with no academic benefit
New $22/credit textbook policy is a rare positive step from administration
Students fear degree devaluation as faculty confidence and quality collapse
key_quotes: I have already lost confidence in the school's executive admin. The 8 week idea comes across as scammy
$22 is nothing compared to the thousands of dollars you pay for 8-week courses for an admin that doesn't even listen
I pray this does not devalue my degree. This school needs an administration overhaul

key_points: Students show strong community solidarity after tragedy but deep distrust of leadership
Campus culture is resilient and diverse; ROTC cadets widely praised as heroes
Pervasive sense that ODU is 'falling from grace' under current administration
key_quotes: First semester here, and truly, garbage ass administration. I really liked most of my teachers
I'm graduating this semester and this school is an absolute abomination
Out of all the great public universities in Virginia, ODU was the best choice for us academically, socially, and price-wise

key_points: **Campus Safety**: Constant Hall shooting killed Lt. Col.; arena unlocked, lights broken
**Administration**: Faculty 41-7 no-confidence vote ignored; president extended 5 years anyway
**Academics**: All online courses forced to 8-week format; professors say workload is impossible to compress
key_quotes: University leadership is intimidating, suspending and threatening to fire anyone who speaks up or criticizes the admin openly
They are changing it halfway through to 8-week asynchronous. I don't think I can trust ODU for future terms
My friend literally couldn't enroll in classes without completing the [criminal history] form

key_points: Geography and Communications departments have standout, passionate professors
ODU's Hampton Roads location gives real access to military, government, and maritime careers
Strong alumni loyalty in region; being an ODU grad gets your foot in the door locally
key_quotes: Dr. Timothy Kidd in Geography — by far the best class I ever took at ODU
As a hiring manager and ODU alum, an ODU grad almost always gets an interview from me
Diversity and cybersecurity program was not intense math — loved that
